EnvelopeArtifact.description: Single bundle artifact with SHA-256 digest.
EnvelopeArtifact.title: EnvelopeArtifact

description:
Top-level envelope written as ``evidence_envelope.json``.

The ``subject`` is a free-form dict carrying the case-specific payload
(certification verdict, gateway claims, handoff manifest, ...). Subjects
are themselves schema-tagged via the legacy schema names, so consumers
can route on either ``envelope_kind`` (preferred) or
``subject["schema"]`` (legacy compat).

envelope_kind.description: Dispatch case: 'certification' (ADR-012), 'gateway_airgap' (gateway air-gap validation), or 'handoff' (operator export pack).
envelope_kind.title: Envelope Kind

reproducibility.description: Legacy smrforge.reproducibility_block.v1 mirror of ``runtime_identity`` -- kept for one release for backward-compatible readers; will be removed alongside the deprecated schema (ADR-014).
reproducibility.title: Reproducibility

runtime_identity.description: Canonical smrforge.runtime_identity.v1 block (ADR-014). Single source of truth for who/what/where/when produced this envelope.
runtime_identity.title: Runtime Identity

sha256sums_sha256.description: SHA-256 of sha256sums.txt; pins the integrity list.
sha256sums_sha256.title: Sha256Sums Sha256

subject.description: Case-specific payload. Carries its own legacy schema header for downstream consumers that still grep on names like 'smrforge.certification_manifest.v1'.
subject.title: Subject

title: EvidenceEnvelope
